description: Per-CA-case-category UX rules and filing policies. Sparse — T-1.D is not yet started (status: ⏳ per PROGRESS.md). This file captures what is already documented in catalog §5 as the initial baseline; richer per-category rules (DOB requirements, redaction awareness, UD disclaimers, etc.) will land incrementally as T-1.D progresses.

PRB expanded at Step #52 to 20 evidence-backed caseCategoryCodes (Step #49: 2 codes; Step #52: 18 codes covering Trust/Guardianship/Spousal Property/Minor's Claim/Fact-of-Birth/Death/Marriage/Letters-of-Admin/Special-Admin/Determine-Succession/Mgmt-Dispo-Prop-Spouse/Auth-Med-Trtmnt-Adult/Affidavit-Small-Value/Summary-Petition/Ancillary/Probate-of-Wills-Ltrs-of-Admin/Authorization-to-do-Admin + LPS-603110-cross-classified). Step #52 RETRACTED Step #51's '+511110' addition — that was an InfoTrack `Case type:` display-field misinterpretation (the display shows caseTypeCode umbrella, not caseCategoryCode). The remaining 3 categories (JUV, CRI, APP) are STRUCTURALLY BLOCKED AT MADERA EFILE per Step #51 — see `step51EFileReachabilityAudit` block. JUV: zero results for both W&I 300 + W&I 602 searches (likely W&I §827 sealed-record routing). CRI: true criminal codes (911180/911190) absent from the Madera eFile category dropdown entirely. APP: only 'Appeal-flavored' eFile category is Labor Commissioner Appeal which is CIV/Employment-coded; true appellate filings go through a separate state-level appellate eFiling system. The 5/8 figure is the CEILING at Madera. Closing JUV/CRI/APP requires evidence from a non-Madera court (LASC etc.). The overall awaitingEvidenceOverall flag flipped from true → false at Step #49 since the majority of categories are now backed; per-category awaitingEvidence flags for JUV/CRI/APP remain true with the reframed semantics 'awaiting non-Madera-court evidence' per Step #51. Step #56 expanded MEN evidence-backed codes from 2 → 5 via direct legalhub probe (added 604110 WI3050/3051-Narcotics Addict, 605110 PC2966-Commitments, 609110 WI6600-Sexually Violent Predator) plus rebutted the PC-prefix-implies-CRI hypothesis (PC2966 files under MEN umbrella caseTypeCode=611110 at Madera, not CRI), plus documented 5 Madera-non-applicable categories (601110/602110/607110/608110/612110 all returned 0 InfoTrack results), plus surfaced a dev-server stale-build operational risk for the MMH01221 PC1368-Competency re-probe. See step56MenEvidenceAudit block. Step #53 closed post-#52 menu items #15 (Ancillary disambiguation) + #16 (Protect Minor + Lodged Will probes) as Madera-scoped exhaustion: MPR015007 is the ONLY Madera Ancillary docket (cannot disambiguate from codelist Habeas); InfoTrack returns 0 results for Protect Minor + Lodged Will at Madera. All 3 require non-Madera-court evidence — see `step53MaderaEdgeCases` block. Step #53 makes NO changes to any policies[*].knownCategoryCodes (Step #52 20-code ceiling preserved). Step #54 CLOSED KD-001 (flat CASE_CATEGORY codelist namespace assumption) via Option B — codes moved into the new `JtiCourtCategoryMappings.json` slice file with explicit per-court scoping; resolver `FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode` now takes `(courtId, code)`. See `resolvedKnownDebt_KD_001_FlatCategoryCodeNamespace` for the closure record. Step #57 shifted the canonical evidence methodology from InfoTrack-mediated probing (Steps #52, #56) to direct CMS list-query via the now-exposed `SearchCase.cshtml` category dropdown (backend always supported the filter; UI/controller wire-through is new at #57). Exhaustive Madera category probe over all 38 mapped codes + 21 hypothesized unmapped codes promoted 4 new evidence-backed entries: 411100 (CIV, 8 cases, MCV039455), 213110 (FAM, 25 cases, MFL005686), 606110 (MEN, 985 cases, MMH00133 — supersedes Step #56 needs-re-probe), 612110 (MEN, 1 case, MMH01159 — supersedes Step #56 Madera-non-applicable). Per-category evidence count Madera: CIV 3→4, FAM 5→6, MEN 5→7, PRB 20 (unchanged), UD 5 (unchanged but 407110 has 0 Madera evidence; retained per CCP statute coverage). Madera mapping count 38→42. The 5/8 evidence-backed-categories headline figure unchanged; what changed is the evidence DEPTH within those 5 categories. See `step57AllMaderaCategoriesAudit` block for full probe results + 5 findings. Step #59 ran the exhaustive UNMAPPED-code probe (all 139 codes not yet in categoryCodeToJccc) via direct CMS list-query AND verified every hit at the GetCase DETAIL level (85/85 evidence codes' detail caseCategoryCode == list-level CaseCategoryText, 0 diverge). 63 codes were promoted (Madera mapping count 42→105: CIV 4→46 incl. Small-Claims 711110, FAM 6→23, PRB 20→23 incl. the Step #59-restored 511110/532110/561310, MEN 7→8). The 5/8 evidence-backed-bucket headline is UNCHANGED: 19 searchable CRI criminal codes (e.g. 911190 Felony-Other=10945 cases) + 3 self-typed ADMIN codes (110120 Lodged Will=327, 110210 Sanctions, 120110 Juror OSC) were deliberately NOT promoted — searchability proven but workflow/policy NOT validated, so CRI stays awaitingEvidence. JUV remains structurally blocked (all 14 JUV codes returned 0 via direct CMS, now on authoritative evidence). See `step59UnmappedCategoryPromotionAudit` block for the full evidence table + CRI/ADMIN deferral rationale.

resolvedKnownDebt_KD_001_FlatCategoryCod.resolutionSummary: Codes moved out of `policies[*].knownCategoryCodes` (where they still appear as a redundant cache, overwritten at provider-load time) into the new top-level slice file `JtiCourtCategoryMappings.json` which holds per-court projections `{ courts.<courtId>.categoryCodeToJccc: { code: 'JCCC' } }`. Resolver `JtiFieldSchemaProvider.FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode` now takes `(courtId, code)`. `UdDisclaimerPolicy.RequiresDisclaimer` and the 5 controller call sites + 2 SearchCase.cshtml call sites all updated to thread `courtId` through. The legacy `policy.KnownCategoryCodes` C# property is preserved as a runtime PROJECTION (union of all courts' codes mapped to that JCCC) so existing Step #49/#52/#53 drift-guard tests continue to work without rewrite. Madera is the bootstrap court entry; adding a non-Madera court now requires only adding a new `courts.<courtId>.categoryCodeToJccc` dictionary — no resolver or call-site changes needed.

resolvedKnownDebt_KD_001_FlatCategoryCod.summary_HISTORICAL_PRE54: The `knownCategoryCodes` arrays on each policy (e.g., UD: ['407100','407110','407200','407210','407300']) are flat string lists with no court-id scoping. The resolver `JtiFieldSchemaProvider.FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode(string code)` matches on code-only — no `courtId` parameter. This silently assumes all CA courts share a single CASE_CATEGORY codelist namespace, which directly conflicts with JTI's per-court-policy mandate.
resolvedKnownDebt_KD_001_FlatCategoryCod.jtiSourceQuote: The Journal Technologies case management system (CMS) allows each California Court to configure their system to precisely meet their requirements. This means there can be considerable differences between Courts in areas such as code lists, document metadata, workflows, etc. You must assume that EACH Court will have different Court Policy values from each other. For example, Civil Limited case type code for Los Angeles Superior Court is LC while it's 421110 for Placer Superior Court. Similarly, not only are the document list codes in Placer different than Los Angeles, the case category associations for those documents may be different.
resolvedKnownDebt_KD_001_FlatCategoryCod.jtiSourceUrl: JTI EFM Documentation — General Concepts (vendor-mandated per-court-isolation principle)
resolvedKnownDebt_KD_001_FlatCategoryCod.whyItWorksToday: Production currently services Madera only. The codes we declare (e.g., '407100' UD Commercial, '613110' MH Other) are Madera's per-court CASE_CATEGORY codelist values. Within a Madera-only deployment, no collision is possible.
resolvedKnownDebt_KD_001_FlatCategoryCod.whyItBreaksOnSecondCourt: Per the JTI quote above, LASC uses 'LC' (alpha) for Civil Limited where Placer uses '421110' (numeric). For CASE_CATEGORY specifically, two distinct courts could (a) use the same numeric code for different categories — silent misclassification + wrong UX hooks fire — or (b) use court-specific alpha codes (e.g., LASC 'UD') that the current flat resolver would match across all courts indiscriminately.

step51EFileReachabilityAudit.title: Per-bucket eFile-reachability audit at Madera (closed Step #51, 2026-05-27)
step51EFileReachabilityAudit.trigger: User-provided InfoTrack search-aggregator access enabled batch category-search at Madera's eFile division. Step #49 left 3 categories (JUV / CRI / APP) flagged 'awaitingEvidence: true' with the implicit assumption that user-provided dockets would close them via Tier-B probing, parallel to the CIV/UD/FAM/PRB/MEN promotion path. Step #51 set out to either close those buckets or document why they cannot be closed.
step51EFileReachabilityAudit.method: InfoTrack case-search (https://search.infotrack.com/App/case-search?state=CA&step=case-search) configured with County=Madera + Division=eFile + SearchBy=Category. The eFile-division category dropdown enumerates the SUBSET of California Judicial Council case categories that Madera permits filing through the JTI EFM eFile pipeline. For each candidate category, click Search → wait for JTI EFM backend → inspect results table. InfoTrack displays each result as `[CMS-stored-categoryCode] - [eFile-dropdown-label]`, which is high-value evidence: the leading code is the value GetCase responses return (what our policy resolver matches), and the label is the filing-time submission selection.
step51EFileReachabilityAudit.scope: All 6 user-targeted categories from Step #51 ask: W&I Code 300 (JUV-dependency), W&I Code 602 (JUV-delinquency), Asset Forfeiture (CRI-attempt), Writ of Habeas Corpus (CRI-attempt), Appeal from Labor Commissioner (APP-attempt), WI5350-LPS Conservatorship (PRB-bonus). Full Madera eFile category list captured at `scripts/madera_efile_categories.txt` (106 entries) for future reference.

evidenceDockets.JUV_dependency_W&ICode300: 0 results — InfoTrack returned 'No results found' OR the JTI EFM upstream returned 500 Internal Server Error during search. Either interpretation supports structural-block conclusion: Madera eFile does not surface JUV-dependency cases.
evidenceDockets.AssetForfeiture: 5 user-provided MCV-prefixed dockets (MCV035606, MCV036956, MCV036265, MCV036218, MCV046840). MCV = Madera Civil prefix. Per `scripts/madera_case_category.txt`, code 415110 maps to Asset Forfeiture (in the 4xxxxx CIV range). Already in CIV.knownCategoryCodes from prior evidence — confirms civil asset forfeiture (per H&S §11469) is procedurally civil, NOT criminal, and is appropriately classified under CIV.
evidenceDockets.WritOfHabeasCorpus: 5 user-provided MPR-prefixed dockets (MPR015914, MPR016127, MPR011826A, MPR016177, MPR016176) plus ~25 additional InfoTrack rows displayed `Case type: 511110 - Writ of Habeas Corpus`. STEP #52 CORRECTION: Same methodology error as LPS — the InfoTrack `Case type:` display field shows `caseTypeCode` (umbrella 511110), NOT `caseCategoryCode`. ADDITIONALLY, direct legalhub probe on MPR015914 + MPR016127 + MPR011826A at Step #52 all REDIRECTED to /CourtFiling/SearchCase (23KB redirect-target page, not the SF page) — these dockets are not retrievable via the JTI EFM live SF endpoint (likely sealed or archived per W&I §827 / habeas-record confidentiality). True habeas caseCategoryCode remains UNKNOWN at Madera. Step #51's claim that Madera CMS collapses habeas to 511110 is NOT evidence-backed beyond the InfoTrack display layer.
evidenceDockets.AppealFromLaborCommissioner: 5 user-provided dockets with mixed CIV prefixes (MCV021854, MCV023867, SCV004590, SCV004589, M000264CIV). Per codelist 404110 maps to Appeal from Labor Commissioner (in the 4xxxxx CIV/Employment range). Routes via CIV — confirms Labor Code §98.2 administrative-determination appeals at the trial court are civil filings, NOT true appellate proceedings. True appellate filings (Court of Appeal, Supreme Court) go through a separate eFiling system and are NOT reachable via Madera trial-court eFile.
evidenceDockets.WI5350_LPS_Conservatorship: 5 user-provided MPR-prefixed dockets (MPR10622, MPR011875, MPR7458, MPR012098, MPR7413) plus ~20 additional InfoTrack rows displayed `Case type: 511110 - WI5350-LPS Conservatorship`. STEP #52 CORRECTION: The InfoTrack `Case type:` display field shows `caseTypeCode` (umbrella, 511110 for all PRB-bucket cases), NOT `caseCategoryCode` (what our resolver matches). Direct legalhub probe on MPR10622 + MPR011875 + MPR7458 returned `var caseCategoryCode = '603110'` (MEN-range, NOT 511110, NOT codelist's 532110). PRB.knownCategoryCodes correctly contains 603110 (per Step #52), not 511110 (which Step #51 incorrectly added).

JUV_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.conclusion: JUV is NOT eFile-reachable at Madera. Both W&I Code 300 (juvenile dependency) and W&I Code 602 (juvenile delinquency) — the two highest-volume, most-populated JUV surfaces statewide — return zero results via Madera eFile category search. The JCCC category codes exist in the JTI submission codelist (`scripts/madera_case_category.txt` rows 313110/314110/321110), but the eFile pipeline returns no matching cases. Likely cause: W&I §827 juvenile-record confidentiality requires sealed/separate routing; juvenile cases at Madera are paper-filed or processed through a non-eFile system.
JUV_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.implication: JUV.awaitingEvidence cannot be flipped to false at Madera alone. Closing JUV requires evidence from a non-Madera court (LASC has true JV-prefixed dockets; LASC onboarding would unblock). Reframe: 'awaiting evidence' → 'structurally blocked at Madera eFile'.
JUV_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.parallelTo: Madera MH case-INI block (per Step #49 — Madera doesn't expose Mental Health case initiation through eFile, only SF on existing MH cases). Same architectural pattern: category exists in the codelist but is not eFile-pipeline-reachable.

CRI_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.conclusion: CRI (true criminal cases) is NOT eFile-reachable at Madera. Inspection of `scripts/madera_efile_categories.txt` (106 eFile-permitted categories) shows ZERO entries from the true CRI numeric ranges (911180 Felony Drug, 911190 Felony Other, 921xxx Misdemeanor series, etc.). The 'CRI-flavored' candidates that DO appear in the eFile dropdown all turn out to be civil-coded on inspection: 'Asset Forfeiture' is 415110 (CIV/civil-forfeiture per H&S §11469); 'Writ of Habeas Corpus' has 3 codelist entries but Madera collapses Probate-routed habeas to 511110 (PRB umbrella).
CRI_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.implication: CRI.awaitingEvidence cannot be flipped to false at Madera. True criminal cases (filed by DA's office) don't go through the JTI EFM eFile pipeline — they have their own court-internal CMS path. CRI evidence requires either (a) a court that exposes criminal eFiling (rare; most CA counties don't), or (b) deferring CRI to a future scope.
CRI_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.parallelTo: Same as JUV — the bucket exists in the JCCC namespace but the eFile pipeline doesn't surface it. Possibly related to confidentiality + prosecution-only access model.

APP_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.conclusion: APP (true appellate cases) is NOT eFile-reachable at Madera. The eFile category dropdown's only 'Appeal'-prefixed entry is 'Appeal from Labor Commissioner', which is codelist 404110 — a CIV/Employment-range Labor-Code-§98.2 administrative-determination appeal, structurally a civil filing in superior court. True appellate filings (writs to the Court of Appeal, petitions for review by the California Supreme Court) go through a SEPARATE state-level appellate eFiling system, not the JTI EFM trial-court pipeline.
APP_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.implication: APP.awaitingEvidence cannot be flipped to false at Madera (or any trial-court). APP evidence would require integration with the appellate-court eFiling system, which is out of scope for the current JTI EFM trial-court integration.
APP_structurallyBlocked_atMaderaEFile.parallelTo: Same as JUV/CRI — but with an additional architectural twist: APP would require a different eFiling system entirely, not just a different court.

codelistVsCMS_architecturalDistinction.conclusion: The file `scripts/madera_case_category.txt` is the JTI EFM SUBMISSION codelist (filing-time dropdown codes — what the filer selects when initiating a case), NOT the CMS-stored categoryCode list (what GetCase responses return at SF time). For some categories these match (e.g., 511210 'Probate of Wills' is both submission and CMS storage); for others they diverge (e.g., LPS submission 532110 → CMS storage 511110; habeas submission 412960/614130/911220 → CMS storage 511110).
codelistVsCMS_architecturalDistinction.implication: knownCategoryCodes MUST contain CMS-stored codes only, since `FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode` is called from GetCase response parsing paths (CourtFilingController.LoadCaseDetail, UdAccessAttestationService.GateUdAccessAsync, etc.). Submission codes from the codelist file may NOT be authoritative for runtime resolution — they describe what the filer chose at submit time, not what the CMS stored. This distinction was implicit pre-Step-#51; now it is documented and drift-guarded.

step51EFileReachabilityAudit.conclusion: Madera eFile exposes 5 of 8 JCCC buckets: CIV, UD, FAM, PRB, MEN. The remaining 3 (JUV, CRI, APP) are STRUCTURALLY BLOCKED at Madera eFile — not 'awaiting evidence'. Evidence from non-Madera courts is required to promote them. The 5/8 figure is the ceiling at Madera and should be framed as 'evidence-backed for Madera' rather than 'incomplete'. Additionally, the architectural distinction between JTI EFM submission codelist and Madera CMS-stored categoryCode is now documented: knownCategoryCodes must contain CMS-stored codes (verified via InfoTrack `[code] - [label]` display or live legalhub probe), not submission codes from `scripts/madera_case_category.txt`.

caseTypeCodeVsCaseCategoryCode.summary: NEW architectural distinction documented at Step #52: GetCase responses populate BOTH `caseTypeCode` (umbrella class identifier) AND `caseCategoryCode` (per-subtype CMS storage). They serve different purposes and have different cardinalities.

caseTypeCode.description: Umbrella class identifier — one per top-level case division. Observed values: 511110 (PRB umbrella, all Probate cases), 611110 (MEN umbrella, MMH00187+MMH00192), 421110 (CIV Limited umbrella, e.g., SCV004590).
caseTypeCode.jtiReference: JTI EFM general-concepts doc states 'Civil Limited case type code for Los Angeles Superior Court is LC while it's 421110 for Placer Superior Court' — caseTypeCode is the entity JTI refers to in that quote.
caseTypeCode.role: Top-level routing + analytics. Not what FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode matches against.

caseCategoryCode.description: Per-subtype CMS storage code — one per logical case subtype within an umbrella. Observed values across PRB alone: 20 distinct codes (Step #49 + Step #52 evidence). Other buckets (CIV/UD/FAM/MEN) each have their own subtype range.
caseCategoryCode.resolverUsage: This is what our policy resolver `JtiFieldSchemaProvider.FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode` matches against. Callers: `EFilingMvcController.GateUdAccessAsync` (UD §1161.2 gate trigger), `EFilingMvcController.CaseDetailAjax` (AJAX defense-in-depth), `UdDisclaimerPolicy.RequiresDisclaimer` (the canonical resolver entry-point).
caseCategoryCode.role: Per-subtype routing + UX-hook firing. THIS is what `knownCategoryCodes` must contain.

caseTypeCodeVsCaseCategoryCode.implication: InfoTrack search-results UI displays `caseTypeCode` in the `Case type:` field (not `caseCategoryCode`). InfoTrack is a DISPLAY layer for case lookup; it doesn't expose the same field set our SF parser does. For evidence purposes, only the live SF page's `var caseCategoryCode` JS bootstrap counts.

crossClassification_LPS_603110.summary: LPS Conservatorship has caseCategoryCode 603110 (MEN-range) but caseTypeCode 511110 (PRB umbrella). Cases are MPR-prefixed (Madera Probate Records) and administered through the Probate department.
crossClassification_LPS_603110.bucketAssignment: PRB — docket-prefix + caseTypeCode + administrative-routing all point to Probate. The MEN-range caseCategoryCode reflects the W&I §5350 statutory grounding (LPS is mental-health-statute-driven) but the workflow is Probate-administered.
crossClassification_LPS_603110.policyImpact: No UX hook divergence from regular PRB cases. Per Step #50 (negative finding), W&I §5328 / LPS confidentiality is NOT a JTI-mandated EFSP-side gate, so policy resolution to PRB vs MEN doesn't change UX behavior currently. If LASC or another court ever adopts an MH-confidentiality local-rule, the `appliesToCourts`-scoped rule mechanism handles it without needing to re-bucket LPS.

ambiguity_614130_Ancillary_or_Habeas.summary: Code 614130 appears in `scripts/madera_case_category.txt` as 'Writ of Habeas Corpus' (MEN-range submission code). ambiguity_614130_Ancillary_or_Habeas.hypothesis_A_CMSDoubleUse: Madera CMS uses 614130 for BOTH Ancillary Proceedings AND Writ of Habeas Corpus, with semantics disambiguated by other case attributes (case title, party shape, etc.).
ambiguity_614130_Ancillary_or_Habeas.hypothesis_B_InfoTrackMisclassification: MPR015007 is actually a Habeas case that appeared under InfoTrack's Ancillary search by some indexing quirk. User's docket inventory listed it under Ancillary.
ambiguity_614130_Ancillary_or_Habeas.hypothesis_C_561310_NeverSurfaces: The codelist's 561310 (Ancillary Proceedings) is a SUBMISSION-only code that never lands as a CMS-stored caseCategoryCode; Madera always normalizes Ancillary filings to 614130 at storage time. Mirror of the LPS submission-vs-storage divergence (532110 submission → 603110 storage).
ambiguity_614130_Ancillary_or_Habeas.resolution: Inconclusive at Step #52 with 1 docket. Adding 614130 to PRB.knownCategoryCodes for now (the actual probed CMS value), with the openQuestions entry tracking the disambiguation need. PC1368_Competency.outcome: CASE-NOT-RESOLVABLE-AT-CMS — Re-probe after Step #56's dev-server restart-cycle returned HTTP 200 with a 1-hop redirect to /CourtFiling/SearchCase (response body = SearchCase page, 23,809 bytes, NOT the ~189KB SubsequentFiling page that MMH00135/MMH00612/MMH00201 produced). The redirect is triggered by `EFilingMvcController.SubsequentFiling:410-415` when `IcourtFilingService.GetCaseWithDiagnosticsAsync(courtId, caseDocketId)` returns `caseInfo == null`. InfoTrack indexes MMH01221 (so the case existed at some point), but Madera's CMS doesn't resolve it via the current legalhub session — possibilities: case sealed/restricted (W&I §5328 / PC §1368 confidentiality), case archived after disposition, InfoTrack index stale, or access-permission mismatch on the test user account. Cannot be disambiguated without vendor consultation. Codelist code 606110 stays unobserved at Madera. Status NOT added to MEN.knownCategoryCodes.
PC1368_Competency.finding: This entry replaces an EARLIER Step #56 finding (now retracted) that attributed the failure to a dev-server stale-build runtime view-compilation error. That issue WAS real and WAS observed (`SearchCase.cshtml(17,64): error CS1501: No overload for method 'RequiresDisclaimer' takes 2 arguments` — the dev server was running an EFiling.Nop.dll from 5/21 with the pre-Step #54 1-arg signature) but was RESOLVED during the same Step #56 session via solution rebuild + Nop.Web restart with explicit `ASPNETCORE_URLS=https://localhost:58085;http://localhost:58086` env var (the original 5/21 Visual Studio launch had applied launchSettings.json which a direct EXE-launch silently ignores). After the fix, SearchCase returns 200 cleanly and the re-probe revealed the TRUE underlying status: MMH01221 is unresolvable at Madera CMS, not a Razor-compilation issue.
PC1368_Competency.reProbeMethodologyResolved: Step #56 also resolved the dev-server stale-build operational risk inline: (1) PID 21280 (Nop.Web from 5/21 4:22 PM) killed; (2) `dotnet build nopCommerce/src/NopCommerce.sln` rebuilt Nop.Web's bin folder with the post-Step-#54 EFiling.Nop.dll; (3) Nop.Web.exe relaunched with `ASPNETCORE_URLS=https://localhost:58085;http://localhost:58086` env var (since direct EXE-launch bypasses launchSettings.json — a subtle operational gotcha now logged for future sessions). Total downtime ~5 minutes. Convention to prevent recurrence: `dotnet build src/EFiling/EFiling.sln` (or NopCommerce.sln if Nop.Web's bin is the target) before live probing after any cross-cutting controller/policy signature edit; and `dotnet run --project nopCommerce/src/Presentation/Nop.Web` (or set ASPNETCORE_URLS explicitly) when launching outside VS/Rider.

step56_F4_DevServerStaleBuildSurfacedAnd.summary: Step #56 surfaced a dev-server stale-build runtime view-compilation error AND resolved it inline. Pass 1 of MMH01221's probe hit HTTP 500 because the running Nop.Web process (PID 21280, started 5/21 4:22 PM) was loading EFiling.Nop.dll from 5/21 with the pre-Step-#54 1-arg `RequiresDisclaimer` signature; SearchCase.cshtml's 2-arg call site CS1501-failed at runtime view compilation. Source code was correct since Step #54. Resolution: PID 21280 killed → `dotnet build nopCommerce/src/NopCommerce.sln` rebuilt Nop.Web's bin folder with the current EFiling.Nop.dll → Nop.Web.exe relaunched. SearchCase now returns 200 cleanly; the other 3 dockets (MMH00135/MMH00612/MMH00201) remained unaffected throughout (they render SubsequentFiling.cshtml directly which doesn't reference the stale overload).
step56_F4_DevServerStaleBuildSurfacedAnd.policyImpact: Not a code defect. Operational risk surfaced as a category (build-before-probe convention) AND a secondary operational risk surfaced (direct EXE-launch bypasses launchSettings.json — Nop.Web bound to default port 5000 instead of the launchSettings 58085 until ASPNETCORE_URLS env was explicitly set).
step56_F4_DevServerStaleBuildSurfacedAnd.mitigation: (1) Run `dotnet build src/EFiling/EFiling.sln` (or `NopCommerce.sln` when Nop.Web's bin is the live target) before probing after any cross-cutting controller/policy signature edit. (2) When launching Nop.Web outside Visual Studio / Rider, use `dotnet run --project nopCommerce/src/Presentation/Nop.Web` (which applies launchSettings.json) OR set `ASPNETCORE_URLS=https://localhost:58085;http://localhost:58086` explicitly before `Nop.Web.exe` launch. Direct EXE-launch silently ignores launchSettings.json.
step56_F4_DevServerStaleBuildSurfacedAnd.broaderRisk: The same class of stale-build-during-dev-iteration risk applies to any future KD-001-style signature change. The launchSettings-bypass risk applies any time the dev server is restarted via non-VS/Rider tooling (PowerShell Start-Process, scripts, CI helpers).
step56_F4_DevServerStaleBuildSurfacedAnd.reProbeOutcome: After resolving the stale-build issue, re-probe of MMH01221 revealed the TRUE underlying status: HTTP 200 with 1-hop redirect to SearchCase because CMS GetCase returns null. The original 'needs-re-probe' status is now superseded by 'CASE-NOT-RESOLVABLE-AT-CMS' (see PC1368_Competency evidenceTable entry).

step57AllMaderaCategoriesAudit.title: Exhaustive Madera category-search probe + UI surface promotion + 4 new evidence-backed codes (closed Step #57, 2026-05-29)
step57AllMaderaCategoriesAudit.purpose: Replace the InfoTrack-mediated probing methodology (Steps #52, #56) with direct CMS list-query as the canonical evidence pattern. The backend (`SoapEnvelopeBuilder.BuildGetCaseListRequest` → `<ns1:CaseCategoryText>`) had supported category-filtered case search since the original GetCaseList implementation, but no UI/controller surface exposed it until this step. Step #57 wires the capability through `CaseSearchModel.CaseCategoryCode` → `CourtFilingController.SearchCasesAsync` (new `category` switch case) → `EFilingMvcController.SearchCasesAjax` (new `caseCategoryCode` query param) → `SearchCase.cshtml` (new category dropdown + result column). With this exposed, Step #57 ran an exhaustive probe over all 38 mapped Madera codes (3 CIV + 5 UD + 5 FAM + 20 PRB + 5 MEN — the post-Step-#56 baseline) plus 21 hypothesized unmapped codes (gap-fillers in the CIV/FAM/MEN ranges) to discover any additional evidence-backed categories.
step57AllMaderaCategoriesAudit.method: PowerShell script `.playwright-cli/probe-step57-all-madera.ps1` calls `https://localhost:58085/CourtFiling/api/efiling/search-cases?courtId=madera&searchMode=category&caseCategoryCode={code}` for each candidate code, parses the JSON response, and records (count, sample docket, distinct caseCategoryCodes, distinct caseTypeCodes) per code. The dev server proxies the request through `JtiEFilingProvider.SearchCasesAsync` → `BuildGetCaseListRequest` → SOAP envelope with `<ns1:CaseCategoryText>{code}</ns1:CaseCategoryText>` to the live Madera CMS. Every result the CMS returns has its category fields verified to match the requested filter — establishing that Madera honors the filter and that the SOAP element is misnamed (`CaseCategoryText` expects the numeric codelist code, not the human-readable label).

step57_F2_DirectCmsListQueryIsAuthoritat.summary: InfoTrack's category-search index is NOT authoritative for Madera CMS state. The 612110 InfoTrack-false-negative proves it: InfoTrack returned 0 results, but direct CMS list-query found 1 case (MMH01159). InfoTrack appears to be a derived index (possibly limited to public docket-feed cases) rather than a mirror of the live CMS. Direct CMS list-query via JTI WSDL is the canonical evidence source going forward.
step57_F2_DirectCmsListQueryIsAuthoritat.policyImpact: Step #56's 'Madera-non-applicable' findings for the 5 codes (601110/602110/607110/608110/612110) are now revised: 4 confirmed truly non-applicable (direct CMS = 0); 1 (612110) was an InfoTrack false negative and gets promoted. Future evidence-expansion should rely on direct CMS list-query primarily, with InfoTrack used only as a fallback for codes that direct list-query also returns 0 for (no harm in cross-checking).
step57_F2_DirectCmsListQueryIsAuthoritat.supersedes: Step #56 'Madera-non-applicable' framing for 612110 (now promoted) and the 'needs-re-probe' framing for 606110 (now promoted).

step57_F3_McH01221_PerDocketUnreachabili.summary: Step #56 documented MMH01221 (PC1368-Competency) as CASE-NOT-RESOLVABLE-AT-CMS based on (a) GetCase returning null, (b) the SubsequentFiling endpoint redirecting to SearchCase. Step #57 added a third independent verification path: a category-filtered list-query for code 606110 returns 985 cases — and MMH01221 is NOT in those 985 results. Three independent CMS-access methods all fail to retrieve MMH01221, while all 985 OTHER cases under the same category code are retrievable. The per-docket unreachability is therefore real and orthogonal to the per-code applicability.

step57_F4_NumericCodesRequiredNotLabels.summary: The SOAP element `<ns1:CaseCategoryText>` is misnamed — Madera CMS expects the numeric codelist value (e.g., '613110'), not the human-readable text label (e.g., 'Mental Health-Other'). criSearchableNotMapped.decision: Option 2 (user-directed, 2026-05-29) — document searchability, do NOT promote.
criSearchableNotMapped.rationale: All 19 codes returned live cases AND detail-verified (caseTypeCode 911110/911410/919110/991150/991160 = CRI umbrella), refuting any implication that criminal cases are absent from Madera's CMS — they are present and searchable (e.g. 911190 Felony-Other=10945, 911140 Felony-Assault=9574, 911180 Felony-Drug=9216). Promoting to the rules-free CRI stub would silently apply civil defaults (Plaintiff/Defendant party model) to People-v-Defendant cases — the kind of partial-evidence over-claim Step #52 corrected. CRI stays awaitingEvidence=true; gap to promotion: SF-reachability confirmation + a CRI policy rule-set.

rules.summary: §1161.2 PUBLIC DISCLAIMER — EFSP must display verbatim JTI-mandated text WHEN SEARCHING for Unlawful Detainers (NOT at filing time)
rules.sourceQuote: JTI EFM requires all EFSPs to alert users of their electronic portal/gateway with the following disclaimer when searching for Unlawful Detainers: 'Code of Civil Procedure §1161.2 (a) limits access to unlawful detainer cases. Accessing an unlawful detainer case through this system could provide confidential information regarding the case. By accessing this case, you agree not to disclose, copy, publish, sell, or otherwise use confidential case information you access for any other purpose. Doing so may expose you to legal liability or result in criminal consequences.'
rules.sourceFile: docs/fileing files/Subsequent Filing/General Concepts/Subsequent Filing - General Concepts _ EFM Documentation.html
rules.wireFootprint: NONE — pure EFSP UX obligation. No WSDL element corresponds to this disclaimer.

evidence.layerAEvidence: Step #49 Tier-B probe via playwright-cli on 4 Madera Probate-typed cases (case type 511110 'Decedent's Estate'): MPR11249 + MPR011994 returned caseCategoryCode 511210 (Probate of Wills & for Ltrs Test.); MPR11261 + MPR10298 returned caseCategoryCode 531110 (Conservatorship). All 4 cases loaded SF page successfully — confirms Probate-typed cases support SF round-trip on Madera. Step #52 MAJOR EXPANSION via direct legalhub probe on 17 representative Probate-family dockets (one per InfoTrack-confirmed eFile category) extracting `var caseCategoryCode` from rendered SF HTML. Each yielded a DEDICATED CMS code: MPR012026 → 521110 (Trust), MPR011984 → 541110 (Guardianship), MPR011981 → 551110 (Spousal Property), MPR012027 → 551310 (Minor's Claim), MPR012364 → 551210 (Fact of Birth), MPR011983 → 511410 (Letters of Administration), MPR011521 → 511510 (Letters of Special Administration), MPR012205 → 551211 (Fact of Death), MPR012419 → 551212 (Fact of Marriage), MPR011987 → 551410 (Determine Succession), MPR011677 → 551510 (Mgmt/Dispo Prop-Spouse), MPR012321 → 551610 (Auth Med Trtmnt Adult), MPR012003 → 561110 (Affidavit Property Small Value), MPR011300 → 561210 (Summary Petition), MPR015007 → 614130 (Ancillary Proceedings — ambiguous; same code is codelist's Habeas), MPR011989 → 511310 (Probate of Wills & Ltrs of Admin), MPR012152 → 511610 (Authorization to do Administrative). Step #52 LPS RE-PROBE: 3 LPS dockets (MPR10622, MPR011875, MPR7458) all returned caseCategoryCode 603110 (NOT 511110, NOT codelist's 532110) — Step #51's `+511110` addition was based on the InfoTrack `Case type:` display, which shows `caseTypeCode` (umbrella 511110), NOT `caseCategoryCode`. ALL PRB-bucket cases share `caseTypeCode=511110` (umbrella PRB TYPE code), but each subtype has its own `caseCategoryCode`. Our resolver `FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode` matches on `caseCategoryCode` (per `JtiFieldSchemaProvider.cs:160`), so knownCategoryCodes must contain caseCategoryCode values only. Step #52 RETRACTED the Step #51 `511110` addition and added 18 evidence-backed caseCategoryCode values across the Probate family.

PRB.knownCategoryCodesNote: ALL PRB-bucket cases at Madera share `caseTypeCode=511110` (the umbrella PRB TYPE code — what the JTI submission codelist labels 'Decedent's Estate', but functions as the parent-class identifier for the entire Probate department's case-routing tree). Each subtype has its own dedicated `caseCategoryCode`. Our policy resolver `JtiFieldSchemaProvider.FindPolicyByCourtCategoryCode` (called from `EFilingMvcController.GateUdAccessAsync` + `CaseDetailAjax` + `UdDisclaimerPolicy.RequiresDisclaimer`) matches on caseCategoryCode (not caseTypeCode), so knownCategoryCodes MUST contain only caseCategoryCode values. Step #52 provided direct evidence for 18 dedicated caseCategoryCodes via Tier-B probe (extracting `var caseCategoryCode` from the rendered SF HTML) — full code-to-subtype mapping: 511210 (Probate of Wills & for Ltrs Test., Step #49), 511310 (Probate of Wills & Ltrs of Admin), 511410 (Letters of Administration), 511510 (Letters of Special Administration), 511610 (Authorization to do Administrative), 521110 (Trust), 531110 (Conservatorship, Step #49), 541110 (Guardianship), 551110 (Spousal Property), 551210 (Establish Fact of Birth), 551211 (Establish Fact of Death), 551212 (Establish Fact of Marriage), 551310 (Minor's Claim), 551410 (Determine Succession Real Property), 551510 (Mgmt/Dispo Prop-Spouse No Capacity), 551610 (Auth Med Trtmnt Adult w/o Conserv), 561110 (Affidavit Property Small Value), 561210 (Summary Petition). Two additional codes added with cross-classification caveats: 603110 (LPS Conservatorship — MEN-range CMS code on MPR-prefixed dockets administered through Probate department; cross-classified bucket assignment per docket prefix + caseTypeCode), and 614130 (Ancillary Proceedings — single docket MPR015007 returned this; same code is codelist's Habeas Corpus mapping, so the assignment carries ambiguity — could be Madera-CMS-double-uses-614130 OR the InfoTrack search was off-by-one for that single case). KEY METHODOLOGY FINDING (Step #52): Step #51 incorrectly added `511110` based on InfoTrack search-results display field `Case type: [code] - [label]` — that display field is the `caseTypeCode` (umbrella TYPE code, 511110 for all PRB cases), NOT the `caseCategoryCode` (per-subtype CMS storage code). Step #52 retracted the `511110` addition and replaced it with the 18 evidence-backed dedicated codes. Going forward, knownCategoryCodes additions must be backed by direct `var caseCategoryCode` extraction from the rendered SF HTML (live legalhub probe), NOT InfoTrack visual display fields. Codelist file `scripts/madera_case_category.txt` is the JTI EFM SUBMISSION codelist (filing-time dropdown — what the filer selects when initiating); it sometimes diverges from CMS storage (notably LPS: submission 532110, CMS storage 603110). NOT YET PROBED for caseCategoryCode evidence: 551710 (Protect Proceeding Involving Minor — 0 user-provided dockets), 561310 (Ancillary Proceedings — only 1 user-provided docket landed on 614130, leaving 561310 unexercised), 110120 (Lodged Will — 0 user-provided dockets). True Habeas Corpus caseCategoryCode also UNKNOWN: the 5 Step #51 user-provided Habeas dockets (MPR015914 etc.) all redirect to SearchCase via the SF endpoint at Madera (likely sealed/archived/not-retrievable), so no direct caseCategoryCode evidence exists for habeas yet — Step #51's '511110 - Writ of Habeas Corpus' InfoTrack display was the caseTypeCode umbrella, not the caseCategoryCode.

resolvedOpenQuestions.question: Confidentiality / sealed-record gate at MH SF surface? Investigate whether W&I Code §5328 or LPS confidentiality requires an EFSP-side disclaimer parallel to UD §1161.2.
resolvedOpenQuestions.resolution: NEGATIVE FINDING — no JTI-mandated MH confidentiality gate exists. See top-level `step50ConfidentialityGateAudit` block for the doc-scan methodology + per-category findings. Madera Tier-B observation (Step #49) is consistent with JTI mandate: MMH00187 + MMH00192 both load SF without any attestation redirect because JTI does not require one. W&I §5328 governs clinical-record confidentiality at the institutional level but is NOT translated by JTI EFM into an EFSP-side disclaimer obligation. If a court eventually adopts an MH-specific disclaimer per local rule, it would land as a `appliesToCourts`-scoped rule (analogous to LASC COVID-19 or Ventura UD-Limited), not a universal MEN policy rule.

The Step #56 finding that MMH01221 specifically was CASE-NOT-RESOLVABLE-AT-CMS remains true — it's not in the 985 results either, confirming per-docket unreachability — but the IMPLICATION that code 606110 itself was Madera-non-applicable was wrong. rules.summary: Juvenile (3-code CaseTypeText) — sensitive party information is REDACTED SERVER-SIDE by JTI EFM (not an EFSP obligation)
rules.sourceQuote: For Juvenile cases (3-code), sensitive party information is masked before the data is sent to external systems: CaseTitleText → 'REDACTED'; PersonGivenName → 'Confidential'; PersonMiddleName → '' (empty); PersonSurName → '' (empty); JudgmentName → 'Confidential'. Criminal cases (9-code) are not redacted but will follow the same structural pattern at the case level.
